themes: model competition (Kimi K2.6, GPT-5.5/Spud pretraining shift)
Google DESIGN.md open-source counter to Claude Design
distribution tools for indie devs (Higgsfield Marketing Studio)
AI product strategy & utility vs. delight gap

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: Credible leak that both GPT-5.5/Spud and Anthropic Mythos are shifting back to pretraining gains over test-time compute — the biggest architectural signal about next-gen model strategy in weeks.

worthClicking.author: @levie
worthClicking.why: Aaron Levie calls the new ChatGPT workspace agents 'the biggest news yet in software going headless' — with a live demo showing full tool + data access, this is a direct threat to every enterprise SaaS AI roadmap.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Concrete framing: OpenAI's workspace agents compress every SaaS company's AI roadmap by 18 months, with the Notion Custom Agents pattern now at mainstream scale.

worthClicking.author: @DataChaz
worthClicking.why: Google open-sourced DESIGN.md as a direct counter to Anthropic Claude Design's rate limits — a meaningful competitive move worth tracking for anyone building agent-driven UI tooling.

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: The clearest articulation of Anthropic's self-inflicted PR damage: silence on the pricing blunder is compounding the problem while competitors accelerate.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: 5.7K likes, 522K views — Gemini's coding perception gap is a real market phenomenon worth understanding if positioning against Google's AI stack.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Sharp breakdown of why UGC ad costs ($15K+ for 50 variants) are a distribution kill shot for solo founders, setting up why Higgsfield-style tools matter strategically.

worthClicking.author: @DataChaz
worthClicking.why: Higgsfield's Marketing Studio (Hermes agent + Meta/TikTok hook scraping → 500+ video variants) is the most concrete new distribution tool for indie devs in this batch.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: Useful product strategy framing: utility alone doesn't create retention, and most AI products are missing the play/delight/aesthetic layer — directly relevant to AI product design decisions.

worthClicking.author: @JaynitMakwana
worthClicking.why: K2.6 scoring 85% on Live Code Bench vs Claude's 64% at 7x cheaper is a real pricing and capability signal for anyone building on top of coding models.

voices.why: Consistently early on model release intelligence (GPT-5.5/Spud pretraining shift, Anthropic valuation dynamics) with analysis rather than just amplification.
Covers AI product strategy, PM role evolution, and distribution economics for solo founders with specific numbers — relevant across all three operator interests.
Sharp takes on AI product design (utility vs. delight), Gemini's coding perception gap, and consumer attention — small account but high signal-to-noise.
Practical tooling coverage (DESIGN.md, Higgsfield) aimed at indie builders, with a consistent focus on the distribution and agent-UI workflow problems.

examples: RoundtableSpace all-caps engagement bait (crypto shilling, 'LOCK IN OR GET LEFT BEHIND', Polymarket bot promos)
aakashgupta off-topic threads (salmon/cocaine study, Down syndrome, Ratatouille business history, Red Dead Redemption)
newsletter self-promo posts with zero content
